---
title: "bestfit"
subtitle: "Bayesian flood-frequency and extreme-value analysis in R and Python"
toc: false
---

`bestfit` provides an R package (`bestfitr`) and a Python package (`bestfitpy`) for
stochastic hydrology, flood-frequency, and extreme-value analysis. Both are thin
bindings over a single shared C++17 core that is a faithful port of the USACE Risk
Management Center's [Numerics](https://github.com/USACE-RMC/Numerics) and
[RMC-BestFit](https://www.rmc.usace.army.mil/Software/RMC-BestFit/) C# libraries.

Because both packages run the same compiled code with a bit-exact Mersenne Twister,
seeded results are identical across R and Python, and reproduce the upstream C#
library bit-for-bit. Every ported value is validated against the real C# libraries
by an oracle test suite of more than 4,000 reproduced values.

## What's inside

- All 42 univariate distributions of the Numerics library, with moments,
  density/quantile functions, seeded random generation, and MOM / MLE / L-moment
  fitting.
- Bayesian estimation with eight MCMC samplers (RWMH, ARWMH, DEMCz, DEMCzs, HMC,
  NUTS, Gibbs, SNIS) plus Gelman-Rubin and effective-sample-size diagnostics.
- The full RMC-BestFit analysis layer: Bayesian frequency analysis, Bulletin 17C,
  distribution ranking, mixtures, competing risks, point processes, time series,
  spatial GEV, copulas, rating curves, and predictive checks.
- Multivariate distributions, copulas, bootstrap uncertainty, and the supporting
  statistics toolbox (Grubbs-Beck outlier tests, Box-Cox and Yeo-Johnson
  transforms, plotting positions, Latin hypercube sampling).

## Install

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Python

```bash
pip install bestfitpy
```

```python
import bestfitpy as bf

d = bf.Distribution("GeneralizedExtremeValue", [10849, 5745, 0.005])
d.quantile(0.99)
```

## R

```r
install.packages("bestfitr")
```

```r
library(bestfitr)

d <- distribution("GeneralizedExtremeValue", c(10849, 5745, 0.005))
dist_quantile(d, 0.99)
```

:::

Both packages are pre-release; install from the
[GitHub repository](https://github.com/cameronbracken/bestfit) until they land on
PyPI and CRAN.

## Learn more

- [Worked examples](examples/index.qmd) ported from the official
  [Numerics-Python-Examples](https://github.com/USACE-RMC/Numerics-Python-Examples)
  repository, each shown in both Python and R and checked against the upstream C#
  outputs.
- [Python API reference](reference/index.qmd)
- [R API reference](r/index.html)
